text1:
Developed under the mysterious "Project Mistral", the Nachtreiher appears to be a prototype self-sufficient wanzer design. The cutting-edge reactor design is powered by an experimental "Sakuradite Core Luminous" installed into a heavily modified 9th-gen frame. Increased power output has allowed for the installation of a shield generator alongside a Phase Grazer core for unparalleled mobility, without sacrificing the powerful chest-mounted missile systems signature to the Raven.

Numerous subsystems evoke the design language of Arma Armature's Cataphract designs, but it is unclear if this is due to collaboration between the two corporations, or a blatant theft of proprietary technology. Regardless, this merging of design philosophies gives the Nachtreiher unique capabilities within Diable's fleet; that is, if any specimens entered into military circulation.

Project M.I.S./T.R.A.L.'s namesake vessel, made up of the "Mobile Intercept Strikercraft", the Nachtreiher, and the "Tactical Ranged Assault Lancer", the Vogelheim. The combined "Mistral" unit was an attempt at replicating the Virtuous project by rival factions within Diable corp. It's control interface, a forced co-dependance between a cyber-augmented human and an AI core, ultimately resulted in the loss of the unit. According to logs from the vessel's black box, the test "pilot" and the AI core had orchestrated an escape during routine maintinence, where the AI core had mysteriously remained powered during checks on the loyalty-assurance devices.

A novel redesign of what was previously seen as a novelty of the Diable Avionics fleet, the Discordant was developed as a close-support variant of the Pandemonium-class. Working from an antiquated "FF-based" design, the vast hanger bays and manufactorums were repurposed to house an extensive missile autoforge, extending down the spine of the ship.

The Diable Corporation's acquisition of a rare Arsenal Droneship hull from the Traverser Design Bureau has been a continued source of political tension between the two organizations. According to Diable's public records, the ship was salvaged from an illegal incursion into Diable space, stripped, and scuttled. Rumors of a ship matching the Sunshower's silhouette began circulating circa c+204 under the command of a notable fleet of Diable's comissioned mercenaries. The ship appears to have been radically reconstructed for crewed operations, with launch bays refitted to field Diable's signature Wanzer mechs.

A favorite among Angelos pilots - the few 9th-generation Raven LPCs the Contingent managed to "aquire" from Diable Avionics have been modified from their standard configuration, featuring overclocked weapons and advanced reactor systems, allowing for the installation of a shield emitter alongside the flicker core displacement system. As these "modifications" are technically hacks done on the LPC level, they have only been integrated into the largest hulls of the Angelos fleet, and are not viable for mass deployment.

An ancient pre-collapse design, the "Raindrop"-class frigate was originally designed by Diable Avionics as a competitor to the wolf. Unfortunately, manufacturing fees regarding production rights dropped it out of favor with the sector at large. When dozens of mothballed hulls were discovered in the spacedocks above Iskandar, the Angelos Contingent rechristened it the "Ame" in the language of Kara's native Luddic population, and pressed it into service as a fast scout and escort vessel for their merchant fleets.

Repurposing the internal hanger and cargo spaces of the Rime-class hull, the Typhoon was designed to be a close-support carrier to repair strikecraft under fire. The ship was refit with more advanced fire-control systems in a larger bridge complex, and pushed into service as a mainline cruiser of the Angelos Contingent fleet.

The Stormsurge began as an experimental craft, attempting to fit the complex mechanics of the Phaze Grazer Core onto a Gust hull. After much trial and error, a nanoforge-stable design was released; although it required reallocating internal wanzer microforges and launch bays to do so. The prow hull hardening and stabalization fins gave this design the nickname "mini-mael", after it's much larger cousin.

A further refinement of a Diable Avionics reverse-engineering of the infamous Gulf-class cruiser. The Tailwind is not able to match the raw firepower of the Gulf, but makes up for it with it's streamlined production process, and it's strikecraft-support technologies provided by Arma Armatura.

The Sunrain is a result of extensive technological cooperation between the Angelos Contingent and the Traverser Design Bureau. In exchange for access to several of Diable Avionics' proprietary designs seized by Angelos, the TDB contributed several capital-sized droneship hulls to the Contingent's military. The automated systems served to offset Angelos' lack of manpower, and the former "Sunshower" hulls were re-armored and refitted for heavier deployments of Wanzer squadrons.

The Sparrow is a carrier-independant refit of an existing 8th-generation wanzer chassis. Equipped with modular weapon systems and a downsized drone defense system from the Calm, the Sparrow is durable enough to act as a fire-support escort vessel and compliment to it's carrier-bound cousins.

Initially part of the proposed 10th-generation wanzer line, the Hummingbird was never approved for mass production. It's miniaturization of the Virtuous's Phase Grazer Siphon was too complex to be incorporated into existing Diable Avionics carrier-bound microforges.

Closer to a band of rebel pilots than a formal polity, the Angelos Contingent boldly announced themselves to the sector through a series of "terrorist" acts against the Diable Avionics Corportation's claims in the former ESO 75 star system. Despite their age, the Contingent found themselves easy allies born from the many enemies of Diable Corp. Connections with the Luddic Church (as well as generous technological collaboration agreements) have gotten the group's "rebellion" in the good graces of political, industrial, and civilian spheres alike.

The stated goal of this rebellion? Complete freedom for their countrymen, and the destruction or reformation of the Diable Avionics military industrial complex. Most established polities of the sector have not formally acknowledged the independance of the Angelos Contingent, but shipments, trade, and advanced weapons find their way to Angelos docks all the same. While It has not yet publically escalated into open conflict, this proxy war has the potential of igniting cycles-old tensions, and giving opportunistic actors an opening to benefit from the conflict.

Design improvements have allowed for the installation of a full-sized Burchel Beam Repeater. Due to it's close proximity to the ship's reactor, the weapon can be used with no impact to the strikecraft's flux grid.
Reactor protocols from the 9th gen "Nightingale" have been integrated into a much larger frame, although the signiture "chirping" remains.
text3: Boosts ballistic weapon rate of fire, energy weapon damage, and mobility by 50% for a short duration.

text1: Reactor protocols from the 9th gen "Nightingale" have been carried over into the Nachtreiher, along with it's signiture "chirping" calling card.
text3: Boosts ballistic weapon rate of fire, energy weapon damage, and mobility by 50% for a short duration.

text1: A redesign of Traverser "Pressurized Shield" technology already aboard the vessel to match Diable corp's existing shield systems.
text3: Reduces the damage taken by shields to a minimum for a short duration.

text1: According to public record, this now-abandoned station was originally registered as a Diable Avionics solar monitoring satellite "ESO 75a". A recent battle has left most functions inoperable, with databanks purged and docks empty.

This lush ocean world was once hidden among the three stars of the Angelos system, and treated as a refuge from the oversight of the Church by its Luddic settlers. Few pilgrims would make the journey, and the planet would be nothing more than a collection of simple fishing villages for dozens of cycles, until updated survey data brought the system into the purview of other major powers within the sector.
text3: Your approach is met with little fanfare and kind luddic greetings. You are only given landing clearing for a small domain-standard starport. It seems transport to any of the more remote locations of the planet must be done through local channels, or on foot.

text1: Iskandar did not have a registered population prior to the activities of the Angelos Contingent. The extent of infrastructure present on the surface and in orbit point to years of industrial development, with reports theorizing the facilities could have been a hidden military base, R&D blacksite, or tug-transported station. Diable Avionics' information control efforts regarding the topic point to one fact; the corporation wishes this moon had forever remained a dead rock to the rest of the sector.
text3: The small moon appears less than a faction capital and more a coordinated military base. Facilities are visible from the ground, and the orbital station's docks are stocked with freshly (re)-painted hulls in the style of Diable Avionics. Red white and blue adornments have been tagged over with the purple and gold moon and star iconography of the Angelos Contingent. Notably, the station itself still bears the red of the former owners.

text1: The moon "Otoha" was officially registered on c+201.05.14 as a mining colony under the title of ESO 75-3a under Diable Avionics colonial charters. To the sector at large, it was obvious that the hellish environment of this colony was specifically chosen for the site of a new prison complex. Acting as a symbol to deter any would-be lawbreaker, It's relative isolation allowed for minimal oversight, even from Diable's own security forces. The complete lack of rumors regarding the operations or even name of the facilities gave it the nickname "The Singularity".
text3: Your fleet is denied acess to the surface, directed to a small resupply port on the orbital station. Any further attempts at gaining information are met by polite corporate rejections, citing "Diable corporate policy".

text1:
Wazabi, one of the most iconic custom made Wanzers of the Orange Comet, Charlotte, comes alive in the 1/144 Real Grade format! From the feature film "Mobile Suit Wanzer: Charlotte's Counterattack" this large orange Wanzer features complex detail as well as gimmicks that allow it to perform a wide variety of dynamic poses. Special attention has been made to ensure both surface detail and also functional detail not seen before in previous interpretations of the Wazabi including pop out and deployment mechanisms are included. Numerous moving parts are present including drones with opening fins, a head that can open to reveal the cockpit, muzzle recoil action on the Hexafire rifle, pistons that move in conjunction with other parts and open maintenance hatches. Includes 6 hands and one right handed trigger hand for a variety of display options.

Technology & Combat Characteristics: After Charlotte returned as the leader of her own Hegemony faction in c+203, she ordered her engineers to create a new Wanzer which would fit her piloting skills and Starfarer powers. The result was the MSN-04 Wazabi, which was later built by the Jangala-based contractor Altair Exotech. With an overall height of 25.6 meters, the Wazabi is much bigger than most standard Wanzers, but thanks to the use of lightweight armor materials and a great number of thrusters it is much faster and maneuverable than other suits. The suit is armed with an abdomen-mounted scattering mega particle cannon, a Hexafire rifle, a pair of beam sabers stored in the arms and a shield with three missiles as well as a beam tomahawk. Because Charlotte is a Starfarer, the Wazabi was also equipped with six drones which the pilot could control through her psycowaves via a Psycommu system. The Wazabi was also the first Wanzer to be equipped with the revolutionary new Psycho-Frame cockpit. This system featured microscopic psycommu receiver circuits installed throughout a mobile suit's internal frame and cockpit walls. These tiny circuits amplify and transmit a Starfarer pilot's psycowaves, enabling the mobile suit to respond instantaneously to mental commands. Armaments: Beam Saber - The Wazabi is equipped with a pair of beam sabers, one stored in each forearm for quick deployment in combat. The beam sabers serve as one of two close combat weaponry of the Wazabi and is a beam of plasma contained by an I-field that can cut through any metal alloy. Drones - One of the Wazabi's powerful weapons is its Starfarer-use remote weaponry - drones. The Wazabi stores six drones in its backpack-mounted storage pods, each with a power rating of 10.6 MW. Hexafire Rifle - The Hexafire rifle is a hand-carried ranged beam weapon that was designed to be used by the Wazabi. It is noticeably larger than most other beam rifles, is powered by a rechargable energy cap, and has a power rating of 10.2 MW. A unique feature of this rifle is that it has two barrels, one for firing normal beam shots and another for firing a scattershot-like beam blasts. The latter is similar to what is fired by a shotgun, the wider beam spreads the damage over a greater area. Shield - The Wazabi is equipped with a shield on its left forearm. The shield mounts three small missiles that can heavily damage a mobile suit, but not a battleship. The shield also holds the storage rack for the Wazabi's beam tomahawk. Beam Tomahawk - Stored on the back of the shield, the beam tomahawk is the Wazabi's most powerful melee weapon and mounts three beam emitters, two at the sides that generate beam axe-like blades, and one at the center that generate a long beam blade. When conserving energy, only one of the side emitter would be used, enabling it to function as a beam axe. At maximum output, all three emitters are used simultaneously, creating a single large beam saber. Special Equipment & Features: Psycho-Frame Cockpit - The Psycho-Frame is a technology that builds the brainwave-to-machine code translator known as Psycommu into the atom-level of the metal frame of a Wanzer. This allows a Starfarer pilot to control the mobile suit as if it were his/her own body. Psycommu System - A Psycommu is a device connected directly into the user's thoughts, through which the user can manipulate it by using his/her mind. The name derives from psychic and communicator. In essence, a psycommu system works by passively scanning the brainwaves of Starfarers, amplifying them in order to send the brainwave signal out long distances, and then translating these brainwaves into raw machine codes upon being received by the selected object. Since brainwaves are not affected by the radio interference property of Minovsky particles, at first it was mainly used to mentally control remote weapons, usually mounted with a single beam gun. This enabled the resurrection of BVR engagements, with bits and drones being the first weapons able to engage targets without visual contact since the development of the Minovsky particle. Later on, the psycommu system would also be used to increase response times. History: After Charlotte, the legendary Hegemony ace, reappears in the early c+200s and establishes the second Hegemony movement, Charlotte commissioned Altair Exotech to produce a custom-designed Wanzer specifically for her Starfarer abilities and superb piloting skills. Painted in her trademark orange color scheme and insignia. The Wazabi was used by Charlotte to personally lead the Hegemony forces in the battle against Diable Avionics' Londo Bell Task Force and especially against her old archrival Damuro Ray and his RX-93 ν Wanzer, which was also equipped with a Psycho-Frame system. However, it would be more accurate to say she led defensive actions against Londo Bell. As Charlotte's Hegemony did not have the manpower or the resources to fight extensive battles to claim territory, they instead relied on the strategy of accelerating asteroids and dropping them on Sieve to create a nuclear winter. Londo Bell would launch its forces and attempt to destroy the asteroids, but they were aggressively guarded by the AMS-119 Geara Doga mobile suits of the Hegemony, led by the MSN-03 Jagd Doga and Charlotte's MSN-04 Wazabi. In one of the earlier asteroid drops, Charlotte would use her MSN-04 Wazabi to battle Damuro Ray in his RGZ-91 Re-GZ, however, the Re-GZ was not able to keep up with the more powerful Wazabi and was nearly destroyed. Afterwards, Charlotte supplied the Altair Exotech with the Psycho-Frame system, which is then used in the RX-93 ν Wanzer they were building for Damuro. As the Hegemony was trying to drop the asteroid Axis, the two would duel again in equal machines. The battle would carry them across, and through Axis in a battle reminiscent of their final duel in the Second AI War. Although Charlotte was an excellent pilot she was ultimately defeated by Damuro who severely damaged the Wazabi and captured Charlotte's escape pod. The wreckage of the Wazabi would remain on the outer surface of Axis for years. Later, Altair Exotech used data from the Wazabi and the ν Wanzer as a basis for the MSN-06S Winanju Stein, which later give rise to the MSN-06S Winanju and RX-0 Unicorn Wanzer.
